commands.repair_reason: Protected maintainer/MEMBER state, dirty mergeability, and compatibility-sensitive model-routing overlap need human/author handling rather than an automated repair lane.; Cleared: The diff touches UI/gateway/model-routing code and tests but does not add dependency, workflow, secret, package, or third-party code-execution changes. commands.review_summary:
The PR refactors Control UI exec/plugin approval prompt state handling, adds neutral dismissal for approval modals, refreshes pending approval lists after stale resolve errors, and adjusts model manifest/default-alias normalization paths.

PR surface: Source +308, Tests +625. Total +933 across 19 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path for the stale Control UI behavior: current main removes resolved approvals from the queue but does not clear active modal error state, and all resolve errors are rendered as modal errors. I did not run the UI flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer/MEMBER state, dirty mergeability, and compatibility-sensitive model-routing overlap need human/author handling rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: Fix stale approval prompts in Control UI
target.body:
Summary:
- Fix stale Control UI approval prompts when an exec/plugin approval is resolved from another client, such as Discord, Slack, CLI, or another Web UI.
- Reuse manifest metadata during model visibility/catalog normalization so Control UI startup RPCs do not block the gateway during manual testing.
- Preserve configured model alias resolution when the selected default includes an auth profile suffix, such as `kimi@nvidia:default`.
- Treat stale resolve responses as terminal UI cleanup signals: `APPROVAL_ALREADY_RESOLVED`, `APPROVAL_NOT_FOUND`, and `unknown or expired approval id` now dismiss the prompt and refresh pending approvals.
- Add a real Dismiss/close path that hides the modal without denying or approving the backend approval; approve/deny actions target the first non-dismissed visible prompt.

Bug summary:
A web approval modal could remain open after the same approval was resolved elsewhere. Acting on that stale modal could leave the prompt showing an error instead of dismissing it. The modal also needed a local close path so the user is not trapped in the approval dialog.

Root cause:
On main, `exec.approval.resolved` only removed the queue item and did not clear active modal error state. The approve/deny handler also treated all resolve errors as modal errors, so backend stale-approval responses stayed visible instead of clearing the stale prompt. The modal cancel path was wired to denial, so closing was not a neutral UI action.

Fix summary:
The approval controller now owns resolve and modal visibility cleanup. Success removes the active prompt. Stale backend errors remove it, refresh `exec.approval.list` and `plugin.approval.list`, and schedule expiry cleanup for refreshed items. A dismissed-id set lets users close the modal locally while keeping the backend approval pending; resolved/expired/refreshed state prunes that set.

The model resolver now strips auth profile suffixes before checking configured default aliases and OpenRouter compat aliases. That keeps alias `kimi` pointing at its configured provider/model even when the selected default is written as `kimi@nvidia:default`.

Bug existed on main:
Yes. The worktree started from `origin/main` and the added regression failed before the fix. The rebased parent still had the same behavior: `removeResolvedApprovalRequest` only removed from `execApprovalQueue`, and `handleExecApprovalDecision` caught every resolve error as `execApprovalError`.

Verification:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/exec-approval.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.browser.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` passed, 87 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-selection-allowed-manifest.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-manifest-workspace.test.ts src/config/model-alias-defaults.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-browse.test.ts src/agents/model-ref-shared.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts` passed, 14 files / 115 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-selection-resolve.test.ts` passed, 4 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/exec-approval.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.browser.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-resolve.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-allowed-manifest.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-manifest-workspace.test.ts src/config/model-alias-defaults.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-browse.test.ts src/agents/model-ref-shared.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts` passed, 19 files / 206 tests.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ...changed files...` passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/model-selection-shared.ts src/agents/model-selection-resolve.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.ts src/agents/model-selection-allowed-manifest.test.ts src/config/defaults.ts src/config/model-alias-defaults.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.ts ui/src/ui/app-view-state.ts ui/src/ui/app.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/exec-approval.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/exec-approval.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.test.ts` passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-ui.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` passed: no accepted/actionable findings.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` first found the model alias auth-profile regression; fixed in `4791d3eb74`.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` passed after the fix: no accepted/actionable findings.
- Testbox-through-Crabbox `tbx_01ksef8gc7n5vr2w375txr8d0n`: `pnpm check:changed` passed.

Behavior addressed: stale Control UI exec/plugin approval modals after external resolution, neutral modal dismissal without resolving the backend approval, and configured default aliases still resolving when an auth profile suffix is present.
Real environment tested: local Vitest/tsgo/oxlint in the OpenClaw worktree, Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox, and local manual Gateway with Discord + Control UI at `http://localhost:19171/`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/exec-approval.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.browser.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/exec-approval.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.browser.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-resolve.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-allowed-manifest.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-manifest-workspace.test.ts src/config/model-alias-defaults.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-browse.test.ts src/agents/model-ref-shared.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json -- CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=6 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 pnpm check:changed`.
Evidence after fix: focused tests passed; Testbox `tbx_01ksef8gc7n5vr2w375txr8d0n` exited 0; live manual logs showed the stale modal closing on the second Discord approval flow; final autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.
Observed result after fix: stale resolve success/errors remove the modal, external resolved events clear active prompt errors, refreshed pending approvals expire from the UI, and Dismiss/Escape close the modal without sending a deny. Configured default alias `kimi@nvidia:default` resolves through alias `kimi` instead of falling back to `openai/kimi@nvidia:default`.
What was not tested: a live Slack-to-Control-UI stale approval flow was not separately run.
